17	// UpdateOptions represents update command configuration
18	type UpdateOptions struct {
19		Check       bool
20		Force       bool
21		Reset       bool
22		Sync        bool
23		Strategy    string
24		Backup      bool
25		Interactive bool
26		Abort       bool
27		DryRun      bool
28		Resource    string // selective update resource
29	}
30	
31	// ConflictInfo represents information about a detected conflict
32	type ConflictInfo struct {
33		FilePath     string
34		LineNumber   int
35		ConflictType string
36		LocalContent string
37		TheirContent string
38		BaseContent  string
39	}
40	
41	// UpdateResult represents the result of an update operation
42	type UpdateResult struct {
43		Success      bool
44		Message      string
45		UpdatedFiles []string
46		Conflicts    []ConflictInfo
47		BackupPath   string
48	}
49	
50	// CommandFactory method - CLI interface layer
51	func (f *CommandFactory) runUpdate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
52		// Extract flags to options struct
53		opts, err := extractUpdateOptions(cmd, args)
54		if err != nil {
55			return err
56		}
57	
58		// Call pure business logic
59		result, err := performUpdate(f.WorkingDir, opts)
60		if err != nil {
61			return err
62		}
63	
64		// Handle output formatting
65		return displayUpdateResult(cmd, result, opts)
66	}
67	
68	// performUpdate checks GitHub for the latest version of each installed plugin
69	// and updates any that are outdated (or all if --force). Always refreshes the
70	// embedded `ddx` skill and the AGENTS.md block so projects that ran `ddx init`
71	// under an older DDx version pick up current skill content without re-running
72	// init. Binary updates are intentionally explicit via `ddx upgrade`; `ddx
73	// update` must not replace a locally-built dogfood binary with the latest
74	// public release.
75	func performUpdate(workingDir string, opts *UpdateOptions) (*UpdateResult, error) {
76		// Refresh the shipped `ddx` skill copy + AGENTS.md block first, regardless
77		// of whether any plugins are installed. This is what lets older projects
78		// pick up new SKILL.md / reference/*.md content without re-init.
79		refreshShippedSkills(workingDir)
80	
81		state, err := registry.LoadState()
82		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
83			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. No packages installed."}, nil
84		}
85	
86		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
87	
88		var updated []string
89	
90		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
91			// Filter to specific target if requested.
92			if opts.Resource != "" && entry.Name != opts.Resource {
93				continue
94			}
95	
96			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
97			if err != nil {
98				continue // not in registry, skip
99			}
100	
101			// Fetch actual latest version from GitHub.
102			latestVersion := pkg.Version
103			if release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source); err == nil {
104				latestVersion = strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
105			}
106	
107			if !opts.Force && entry.Version == latestVersion {
108				continue
109			}
110	
111			// Install the latest version.
112			installPkg := *pkg
113			installPkg.Version = latestVersion
114			newEntry, err := registry.InstallPackage(&installPkg, workingDir)
115			if err != nil {
116				return nil, fmt.Errorf("updating %s: %w", entry.Name, err)
117			}
118			state.AddOrUpdate(newEntry)
119			updated = append(updated, entry.Name+" "+entry.Version+" → "+latestVersion)
120		}
121	
122		if err := registry.SaveState(state); err != nil {
123			return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving state: %w", err)
124		}
125	
126		if len(updated) == 0 {
127			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. All packages are up to date."}, nil
128		}
129	
130		return &UpdateResult{
131			Success:      true,
132			Message:      "Updated: " + strings.Join(updated, ", "),
133			UpdatedFiles: updated,
134		}, nil
135	}
136	
137	// refreshShippedSkills re-copies the embedded `ddx` skill into the project's
138	// skill directories and refreshes the AGENTS.md DDx block. Safe to call on
139	// every `ddx update` because skills.Install with Force=true handles the
140	// "existing files should be updated" case, and generateAgentsMD's
141	// marker-based merge is idempotent. Stale pre-consolidation skill dirs
142	// (ddx-bead, ddx-run, etc.) are swept by cleanupBootstrapSkills.
143	func refreshShippedSkills(workingDir string) {
144		bootstrapSkillNames := []string{"ddx"}
145		for _, dir := range []string{
146			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".agents", "skills"),
147			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".claude", "skills"),
148		} {
149			_ = os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755)
150			cleanupBootstrapSkills(dir, bootstrapSkillNames)
151		}
152		if err := skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, workingDir, skills.Options{Force: true}); err != nil {
153			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: skill install failed: %v\n", err)
154		}
155		generateAgentsMD(workingDir)
156	}

158	// Helper functions for working directory-based operations
159	func extractUpdateOptions(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) (*UpdateOptions, error) {
160		opts := &UpdateOptions{}
161	
162		// Extract flags
163		opts.Check, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("check")
164		opts.Force, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
165		opts.Reset, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("reset")
166		opts.Sync, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("sync")
167		opts.Strategy, _ = cmd.Flags().GetString("strategy")
168		opts.Backup, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("backup")
169		opts.Interactive, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("interactive")
170		opts.Abort, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("abort")
171		opts.DryRun, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
172	
173		// Handle mine/theirs flags by converting to strategy
174		updateMine, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("mine")
175		updateTheirs, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("theirs")
176	
177		if updateMine && updateTheirs {
178			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot use both --mine and --theirs flags")
179		}
180		if updateMine {
181			opts.Strategy = "ours"
182		}
183		if updateTheirs {
184			opts.Strategy = "theirs"
185		}
186	
187		// Check for selective update
188		if len(args) > 0 {
189			opts.Resource = args[0]
190		}
191	
192		return opts, nil
193	}
194	
195	// Output formatting function
196	func displayUpdateResult(cmd *cobra.Command, result *UpdateResult, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
197		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
198		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
199	
200		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
201		writer := out.(io.Writer)
202	
203		// Handle error cases
204		if !result.Success {
205			if len(result.Conflicts) > 0 {
206				return handleConflictOutput(out, result.Conflicts, opts)
207			}
208			_, _ = red.Fprintln(writer, "❌", result.Message)
209			return nil
210		}
211	
212		// Handle check mode
213		if opts.Check {
214			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Checking for updates...")
215			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Fetching latest changes from master repository...")
216			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Available updates:")
217			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Changes since last update:")
218			return nil
219		}
220	
221		// Handle dry-run mode
222		if opts.DryRun {
223			return displayDryRunResult(out, result, opts)
224		}
225	
226		// Display success message
227		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "✅", result.Message)
228		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
229	
230		// Show updated files
231		if len(result.UpdatedFiles) > 0 {
232			_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "📦 Updated resources:")
233			for _, file := range result.UpdatedFiles {
234				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "  • %s\n", file)
235			}
236			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
237		}
238	
239		// Show backup info
240		if result.BackupPath != "" {
241			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "💾 Backup created at: %s\n", result.BackupPath)
242			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
243		}
244	
245		return nil
246	}
247	
248	func handleConflictOutput(out interface{}, conflicts []ConflictInfo, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
249		writer := out.(io.Writer)
250		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
251		cyan := color.New(color.FgCyan)
252		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
253	
254		_, _ = red.Fprintln(writer, "⚠️  MERGE CONFLICTS DETECTED")
255		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
256	
257		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "Found %d conflict(s) that require resolution:\n", len(conflicts))
258		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
259	
260		// Display detailed conflict information
261		for i, conflict := range conflicts {
262			_, _ = red.Fprintf(writer, "❌ Conflict %d: %s (line %d)\n", i+1, conflict.FilePath, conflict.LineNumber)
263			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
264		}
265	
266		// Provide resolution guidance
267		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
268		_, _ = cyan.Fprintln(writer, "🔧 RESOLUTION OPTIONS")
269		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
270		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Choose one of the following resolution strategies:")
271		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
272		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  📋 Automatic Resolution:")
273		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --strategy=ours    Keep your local changes")
274		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --strategy=theirs  Accept upstream changes")
275		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --mine             Same as --strategy=ours")
276		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --theirs           Same as --strategy=theirs")
277		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
278		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  🔄 Interactive Resolution:")
279		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --interactive      Resolve conflicts one by one")
280		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
281		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ⚡ Force Resolution:")
282		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --force            Override all conflicts with upstream")
283		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
284		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  🔙 Abort Update:")
285		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --abort            Cancel update and restore previous state")
286	
287		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "💡 Examples:")
288		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --strategy=theirs   # Accept all upstream changes")
289		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --mine              # Keep all local changes")
290		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --interactive       # Choose per conflict")
291		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --abort             # Cancel and restore")
292	
293		return fmt.Errorf("conflicts require resolution")
294	}

314		} else {

12	// TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks verifies that ddx update --force
13	// replaces legacy symlinks under .agents/skills/ with real files.
14	// This covers the FEAT-015 requirement that skills.Install is always invoked
15	// with Force:true during update (via refreshShippedSkills).
16	func TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
17		homeDir := t.TempDir()
18		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
19	
20		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
21	
22		// Create .agents/skills/ddx as a symlink (pre-migration state).
23		agentSkillsDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills")
24		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentSkillsDir, 0o755))
25	
26		fakeTarget := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "old-global-ddx")
27		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(fakeTarget, 0o755))
28		symlinkPath := filepath.Join(agentSkillsDir, "ddx")
29		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(fakeTarget, symlinkPath))
30	
31		// Verify the symlink exists before update.
32		info, err := os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
33		require.NoError(t, err)
34		require.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "expected symlink before update")
35	
36		// Run ddx update --force; this should call refreshShippedSkills which
37		// calls skills.Install with Force:true, replacing the symlink.
38		_, err = te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
39		// The command may fail due to network operations (checking plugin updates),
40		// but refreshShippedSkills runs unconditionally before any network call.
41		// We tolerate network errors but require the symlink to be gone.
42		_ = err
43	
44		// After update, the symlink must be replaced with a real directory.
45		info, err = os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
46		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
47			// Skill was not installed (embedded FS may not have ddx skill in test);
48			// acceptable — the symlink is gone either way.
49			return
50		}
51		require.NoError(t, err)
52		if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
53			t.Errorf("symlink was not replaced by ddx update --force: %s is still a symlink", symlinkPath)
54		}
55	}
56	
57	func TestUpdate_DoesNotAttemptBinaryUpgrade(t *testing.T) {
58		homeDir := t.TempDir()
59		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
60	
61		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
62	
63		output, err := te.RunCommand("update")
64		require.NoError(t, err)
65	
66		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Checking for DDx updates")
67		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Upgrading DDx")
68		assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
69	}

19	// TestMain clears all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
20	// that tests invoking git commands operate on their own temp-dir repositories
21	// rather than any repository inherited from a hook invocation (e.g. GIT_DIR
22	// set by lefthook during pre-commit). Prefix scrub is important — lefthook
23	// sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS etc. that
24	// also leak into test subprocesses and corrupt the shared repo config.
25	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
26		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
27			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
28				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
29					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
30				}
31			}
32		}
33		cleanupTemp := isolateCmdTestTempRoot()
34		code := m.Run()
35		cleanupTemp()
36		os.Exit(code)
37	}
38	
39	func isolateCmdTestTempRoot() func() {
40		tempRoot, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-cmd-tests-*")
41		if err != nil {
42			return func() {}
43		}
44		oldTmpDir, hadTmpDir := os.LookupEnv("TMPDIR")
45		oldTmp, hadTmp := os.LookupEnv("TMP")
46		_ = os.Setenv("TMPDIR", tempRoot)
47		_ = os.Setenv("TMP", tempRoot)
48		return func() {
49			if hadTmpDir {
50				_ = os.Setenv("TMPDIR", oldTmpDir)
51			} else {
52				_ = os.Unsetenv("TMPDIR")
53			}
54			if hadTmp {
55				_ = os.Setenv("TMP", oldTmp)
56			} else {
57				_ = os.Unsetenv("TMP")
58			}
59			_ = os.RemoveAll(tempRoot)
60		}
61	}

109	// syncDirectory copies files from src to dst, preserving directory structure
110	func syncDirectory(src, dst string) error {
111		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
112			if err != nil {
113				return err
114			}
115	
116			// Skip .git directories from source
117			if info.IsDir() && info.Name() == ".git" {
118				return filepath.SkipDir
119			}
120	
121			// Calculate relative path
122			relPath, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
123			if err != nil {
124				return err
125			}
126	
127			dstPath := filepath.Join(dst, relPath)
128	
129			if info.IsDir() {
130				return os.MkdirAll(dstPath, info.Mode())
131			}
132	
133			// Copy file
134			srcFile, err := os.Open(path)
135			if err != nil {
136				return err
137			}
138			defer func() { _ = srcFile.Close() }()
139	
140			dstFile, err := os.Create(dstPath)
141			if err != nil {
142				return err
143			}
144			defer func() { _ = dstFile.Close() }()
145	
146			if _, err := dstFile.ReadFrom(srcFile); err != nil {
147				return err
148			}
149	
150			return os.Chmod(dstPath, info.Mode())
151		})
152	}

321		te.t.Helper()
322	
323		// Check if we should skip git operations
324		hasNoGitFlag := false

328				break
329			}
330		}

333		if te.GitInitialized && !hasNoGitFlag {

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	)
11	
12	// Command returns a prepared *exec.Cmd for a git invocation that honors
13	// cmd.Dir / -C <path> rather than whatever GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc.
14	// were set in the parent environment.
15	//
16	// Why this wrapper exists (ddx-aa8a5fb3):
17	// Git's repository-discovery rules mean that if GIT_DIR is set in the
18	// process environment, git uses THAT repo and ignores both -C <path> and
19	// the process's working directory for repository selection. ddx commands
20	// invoked from a git hook (lefthook pre-commit, pre-push, etc.) inherit
21	// the hook's GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / GIT_INDEX_FILE. Without stripping
22	// those, any exec.Command("git", ...) inside ddx mutates the hook's
23	// repository (the outer one), producing core.bare=true flips and stray
24	// core.worktree entries in .git/config.
25	//
26	// The wrapper strips exactly git's "local env vars" set (the vars git
27	// itself clears when spawning worktree subprocesses). This is the
28	// canonical set documented by `git rev-parse --local-env-vars`. It
29	// preserves everything else, including GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_TRACE*,
30	// GIT_AUTHOR/COMMITTER_NAME/EMAIL, and custom debugging overrides.
31	//
32	// Dir must be an absolute or working-directory-relative path; it is set
33	// on the returned cmd so that when no GIT_* local vars are in the
34	// environment, git discovers the repo from Dir.
35	func Command(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
36		warnOnce.Do(maybeWarnGitDirLeaked)
37	
38		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...)
39		if dir != "" {
40			cmd.Dir = dir
41		}
42		cmd.Env = CleanEnv()
43		return cmd
44	}
45	
46	// CleanEnv returns os.Environ() with every git local-env-var removed.
47	// Exported so sibling packages (e.g. internal/agent) that spawn NON-git
48	// subprocesses inside a worktree can use the same scrub — those child
49	// processes often invoke git themselves and need the same protection.
50	func CleanEnv() []string {
51		vars := localEnvVars()
52		src := os.Environ()
53		out := make([]string, 0, len(src))
54		for _, kv := range src {
55			eq := strings.IndexByte(kv, '=')
56			if eq < 0 {
57				out = append(out, kv)
58				continue
59			}
60			if vars[kv[:eq]] {
61				continue
62			}
63			out = append(out, kv)
64		}
65		return out
66	}
67	
68	// localEnvVars returns the set of environment variable names that git
69	// treats as repository-local (i.e. scoped to the current git process's
70	// repository selection and cleared when git itself spawns subprocesses
71	// for other worktrees). Result is cached for process lifetime.
72	//
73	// We query `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` once at first use so we pick
74	// up any vars a newer git version adds. If that query fails (git not on
75	// PATH, or restricted environment), fall back to the documented set.
76	func localEnvVars() map[string]bool {
77		localEnvVarsOnce.Do(func() {
78			set := map[string]bool{}
79			// Use exec.Command directly here to avoid recursion through
80			// Command(). This runs with inherited env on purpose: even if
81			// GIT_DIR is set, `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` is a static
82			// list of variable NAMES (not their values), so inherited env
83			// doesn't affect the output.
84			out, err := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "--local-env-vars").Output()
85			if err == nil {
86				for _, name := range strings.Fields(string(out)) {
87					if name != "" {
88						set[name] = true
89					}
90				}
91			}
92			// Fallback / supplement: the canonical set from git's source
93			// (environment.c local_repo_env[]). Kept inline so ddx remains
94			// functional even if `git rev-parse` is unavailable.
95			for _, name := range []string{
96				"GIT_DIR",
97				"GIT_WORK_TREE",
98				"GIT_INDEX_FILE",
99				"GIT_COMMON_DIR",
100				"GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY",
101				"GIT_ALTERNATE_OBJECT_DIRECTORIES",
102				"GIT_PREFIX",
103				"GIT_INTERNAL_GETTEXT_SH_SCHEME",
104				"GIT_SHALLOW_FILE",
105				"GIT_NO_REPLACE_OBJECTS",
106				"GIT_REPLACE_REF_BASE",
107				"GIT_CONFIG",
108				"GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL",
109				"GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM",
110				"GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS",
111				"GIT_CONFIG_COUNT",
112				"GIT_INDEX_VERSION",
113				"GIT_LITERAL_PATHSPECS",
114				"GIT_GLOB_PATHSPECS",
115				"GIT_NOGLOB_PATHSPECS",
116				"GIT_ICASE_PATHSPECS",
117				"GIT_QUARANTINE_PATH",
118				"GIT_INTERNAL_NEW_OBJECT_HOOK",
119				"GIT_IMPLICIT_WORK_TREE",
120			} {
121				set[name] = true
122			}
123			localEnvVarsCache = set
124		})
125		return localEnvVarsCache
126	}
127	
128	var (
129		localEnvVarsOnce  sync.Once
130		localEnvVarsCache map[string]bool
131	
132		warnOnce sync.Once
133	)
134	
135	// maybeWarnGitDirLeaked emits a one-time stderr diagnostic when the
136	// parent environment has GIT_DIR set. This is the canonical smoke signal
137	// for hook-inherited env contamination; surfacing it during normal runs
138	// helps catch upstream leaks we haven't yet wrapped.
139	func maybeWarnGitDirLeaked() {
140		if v := os.Getenv("GIT_DIR"); v != "" {
141			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
142				"ddx git wrapper: stripped inherited GIT_DIR=%q (likely hook context). "+
143					"This is diagnostic; operation proceeds against cmd.Dir instead.\n", v)
144		}
145	}

< ## Starting and monitoring workers in the background
< 
< When the user asks you to **start workers and watch progress**, do
< not invent your own `tail | grep` pipeline — the obvious shapes have
< all bitten in the field. Use these vetted patterns.
< 
< ### One worker, foreground drain
< 
< If the user just wants to drain once and read the result at the end,
< run `ddx work` directly and let it print. Don't wrap with `head`,
< `tail`, or `tee` — pipes to those tools buffer stdout for the entire
< run, so the user sees nothing during execution and everything at the
< end.
< 
< ### One or more workers, watch live
< 
< ```bash
< # Start each worker, one log per worker:
< ddx work > /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>&1 &   # repeat for worker2, etc.
< 
< # Stream with per-worker prefixes (drops the `==>` separator noise
< # that `tail -F` injects when switching files):
< (tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w1: "$0; fflush()}' &
<  tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker2.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w2: "$0; fflush()}' &
<  wait) | grep -E --line-buffered \
<   "^w[0-9]+: (▶|✗|✓|→ |worker exited|attempts:|failed:|landing worktree has|lock_contention|panic|FATAL|excluded|caller_excluded|ladder exhausted|escalation|TriagePowerHint|do route|i/o timeout|connection refused|provider error)"
< ```
< 
< Why those specific tokens — together they cover **every actionable
< event** in a drain:
< 
< - `▶ <bead>` — a new bead was claimed. `✗` and `✓` close it.
< - `→` — outcome arrow, also follows a bead-state transition.
< - `worker exited` / `attempts:` / `failed:` — end-of-drain summary.
< - `landing worktree has` — pre-claim hook hit a stale index; queue
<   is jammed if recovery doesn't kick in.
< - `lock_contention`, `panic`, `FATAL` — infrastructure problems.
< - `excluded`, `caller_excluded`, `ladder exhausted`,
<   `escalation`, `TriagePowerHint` — the escalation path actually
<   firing; absence on a failed bead means the loop is silently
<   no-op'ing instead of climbing the ladder.
< - `do route` — the implementation-phase routing decision, surfaces
<   the provider/model that's about to be hit. Without this in the
<   alternation, the monitor goes quiet during the most interesting
<   window (provider call) and only resurfaces on success/failure.
< - `i/o timeout`, `connection refused`, `provider error` — transport
<   failures. Two in a row to the same `(provider, model)` is the
<   signature of routing that isn't honoring exclusions.
<

description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in\

... [10792 characters truncated] ...

. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:

data.text:
All checks pass and the commit succeeded. 

The implementation adds:
- **`isUpdateTargetDirty`** — checks git status for a single file (ignores untracked `??` entries)
- **`enumerateSkillUpdateTargets`** — lists files `refreshShippedSkills` would write to
- **`collectDirtyUpdateTargets`** — collects dirty files from both skill targets and registry `entry.Files`
- **`backupUpdateFile`** — copies a file to `.ddx/update-backup/<timestamp>/<path>`
- **`--discard-local` flag** on `ddx update` — bypasses the guard (with backup)
- **Atomic refuse** in `performUpdate` — fails before ANY file is written if any target is dirty

Tests cover all four ACs: AC1 (refuse dirty), AC2 (allow clean), AC3 (discard-local with backup), AC4 (partial dirty halts entire update cleanly).
